questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. The jury decided to convict the accused based on the overwhelming evidence presented in court.
questions.explanation: D) Convict(verb) – find guilty, sentence, condemn, imprison, penalize दोषी ठहराना Antonym: Acquit(verb) – Declare someone not guilty, free from charge. (बरी करना) Penalize(verb) – Punish, impose a penalty. (दंड देना) Condemn(verb) – Express disapproval, sentence. (निंदा करना) Sentence(verb) – Declare punishment for a crime. (सजा सुनाना)

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute to fill in the blanks for the given group of highlighted words. The government issued an official order to stop doing business with another country due to escalating political tensions.
questions.explanation: D)Embargo(noun) – An official order to stop doing business with another country प्रतिबंध Oblivion(noun) – the state of being unaware or unconscious of what is happening around one. विस्मृति Corollary(noun) – a proposition that follows from one already proven; a natural consequence. स्वाभाविक परिणाम Aphorism(noun) – a concise statement of a principle or truth. सूक्ति

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Ramgopal is having a very big house in Mumbai
questions.explanation: B) ‘is having' के बदले'has'का use होगा क्योंकि वाक्य Present Indefinite Tense में है और यहां ‘ownership’ (स्वामित्व) को व्यक्त किया गया है। 'Have/Has' का use Ownership या Possession को दर्शाने के लिए Present Indefinite Tense में होता है। 'is having' का use Continuous Tense में होता है जो किसी Temporary Action को व्यक्त करता है, जो यहां सही नहीं है। सही होगा: "Ramgopal has a very big house in Mumbai." ‘is having' will be replaced with 'has' because the sentence is in Present Indefinite Tense, and it expresses ‘ownership.’ 'Have/Has' is used to denote ownership or possession in Present Indefinite Tense. 'is having' is used in Continuous Tense for temporary actions, which is incorrect here. Correct sentence will be: "Ramgopal has a very big house in Mumbai."

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Her strong will helped her overcome every obstacle in her path to success.
questions.explanation: C) Will(noun) – determination, intent, resolve, purpose, desire इच्छाशक्ति Antonym: Weakness(noun) – Lack of strength, feebleness. (कमजोरी) Determination(noun) – Firmness, persistence. (दृढ़ता) Resolve(noun) – Decision, commitment. (संकल्प) Desire(noun) – Wish, longing. (इच्छा)

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. O. Recently, I travelled from the Old Delhi railway station to Dehradun in a two-tier, air-conditioned train compartment. P. I had an upper berth, while the lower berth was occupied by a man. Q. It was a deeply unsettling experience. R. Around 4 a.m., abruptly woken up, I found him standing barely an inch away from me, his face alarmingly close to mine.
questions.explanation: B) OPRQ O starts the paragraph and introduces the primary setting using a clear sentence that sets the scene: “Recently, I travelled from the Old Delhi railway station to Dehradun in a two-tier, air-conditioned train compartment.” P follows O by using a pronoun “I” that connects back to the subject introduced in O. It provides more details about the narrator's position on the train: “I had an upper berth, while the lower berth was occupied by a man.” The use of the pronoun maintains a consistent reference to the narrator and elaborates on the setting. R connects to P with the prepositional phrase “Around 4 a.m.,” which adds a temporal context to the narrative. The phrase “abruptly woken up” indicates a continuation of the narrator's experience and describes an unexpected moment. Q concludes with a statement summarizing the emotional impact of the previous incident: “It was a deeply unsettling experience.” The adjective “unsettling” refers back to the disturbing event described in R, making the connection clear. This conclusion provides a cohesive wrap-up to the narrative.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. His business was so successful that Mark was able to _________ at the age of 50.
questions.explanation: D)'Retire'का use होगा क्योंकि "retire" का अर्थ है कामकाजी जीवन को समाप्त करना और आरामदायक जीवन जीना। sentence में mention है कि मार्क का व्यवसाय इतना सफल था कि वह 50 वर्ष की उम्र में काम छोड़ने में सक्षम था, इसलिए 'retire' यहाँ सही answer है 'Admit' का अर्थ है स्वीकार करना, जो संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'Give up' का अर्थ है हार मानना या छोड़ देना, जो व्यवसाय की सफलता के साथ मेल नहीं खाता। Succeed' का अर्थ है सफल होना, लेकिन यह वाक्य के भाव को पूरा नहीं करता है। Retire'will be used because it means to end one’s working life and live a relaxed life. The sentence states that Mark's business was so successful that he was able to stop working at the age of 50, making 'retire' the correct choice here. 'Admit' means to accept or confess, which is irrelevant to the context. 'Give up' means to quit or surrender, which doesn’t align with the idea of business success. 'Succeed' means to achieve success, but it doesn’t complete the meaning of the sentence correctly.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. Chronic insomnia made it difficult for him to focus during the day due to lack of sleep.
questions.explanation: A) Insomnia(noun) – sleeplessness, wakefulness, restlessness, disturbed sleep, inability to sleep अनिद्रा Synonym: Sleeplessness(noun) – Inability to sleep. नींद न आना Drowsiness(noun) – Sleepiness. तंद्रा Lethargy(noun) – Lack of energy. सुस्ती Slumber(noun) – Light sleep. नींद

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: D) The incorrect spelling is"Choronology", which should be correctly spelled as"Chronology."घटनाओं या तिथियों को उनके घटने के क्रम में व्यवस्थित करना।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Please bring some milk from ______ refrigerator in the kitchen.
questions.explanation: A) ‘The'का use होगा क्योंकि यह "refrigerator" को specify करता है कि यह वही refrigerator है जो रसोईघर में है। 'The' का उपयोग विशेषकर तब किया जाता है जब हम किसी specific चीज़ के बारे में बात कर रहे हों। 'An' का उपयोग तब होता है जब किसी singular noun की बात की जाए जो vowel sound से शुरू होता है। लेकिन यहाँ 'refrigerator' vowel sound से शुरू नहीं होता। 'No article required' गलत है क्योंकि 'refrigerator' के पहले article आवश्यक है। 'A' का उपयोग तब होता है जब हम किसी unspecific singular noun की बात करते हैं। यहाँ refrigerator specific है, इसलिए 'A' गलत होगा। The'will be used because it specifies "refrigerator" as the one located in the kitchen. 'The' is used when we talk about something specific. 'An' is used before singular nouns starting with a vowel sound. However, 'refrigerator' does not begin with a vowel sound. 'No article required' is incorrect because an article is necessary before 'refrigerator'. 'A' is used for nonspecific singular nouns. Here, the refrigerator is specific, making 'A' inappropriate.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The forsaken puppy looked for shelter in the rain.
questions.explanation: A) Forsaken(adjective) – abandoned, deserted, neglected, rejected, forlorn त्यागा हुआ Synonym: Abandoned(adjective) – Forsaken, deserted. (छोड़ा हुआ) Populated(adjective) – Inhabited, lived-in. (आबाद) Thriving(adjective) – Flourishing, prosperous. (फलता-फूलता) Crowded(adjective) – Full of people. (भीड़-भाड़ वाला)

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: C) The incorrectly spelled word in the given options is ‘Squadroun’. The correct spelling is ‘Squadron’, which means “a unit of armed forces, especially an operational unit in an air force or a large group of people or things.” इसका अर्थ है “सैनिक टुकड़ी, विशेष रूप से वायु सेना की एक परिचालन इकाई या लोगों या चीजों का बड़ा समूह।”

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence He began to search unsystematically and untidily through the old boxes in the attic, hoping to find his childhood photographs buried somewhere under the clutter.
questions.explanation: D)Rummage(verb) – To search unsystematically and untidily through something. तलाशना Proletariat(noun) – The working-class people, especially in the context of Marxist theory. सर्वहारा Chimera(noun) – A thing that is hoped for but is illusory or impossible to achieve. कल्पना Clemency(noun) – Mercy or lenience shown towards an offender. दया

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. Shakespeare’s famous sonnet, "Shall I compare thee to a summer’s day?", is beloved by many.
questions.explanation: B) Sonnet(noun) – poem, verse, lyric, composition, rhyme चौदह पंक्तियों की कविता Synonym: Poem(noun) – Verse, sonnet, composition. (कविता) Novel(noun) – A long fictional story. (उपन्यास) Essay(noun) – Short piece of writing. (निबंध) Speech(noun) – Formal address. (भाषण)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the highlighted idiom. If you're a manager, you can't afford to take your eye off the ball for one minute
options: To stop paying attention for a moment
Take an advice from a wrong person
Deteriorating and headed for complete disaster
questions.explanation: A) Take your eye off the ball(phrase) – To stop paying attention for a momentएक पल के लिए ध्यान देना बंद करना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Without strong public support, you end up with (A) / weak governments and beleaguered civil servants, (B) / including the much vaunted police force, being caught (C) / daily in the media in an acusatory meat grinder. (D)
options: Without strong public support, you end up with
weak governments and beleaguered civil servants,
including the much vaunted police force, being caught
daily in the media in an acusatory meat grinder.
questions.explanation: D) 'acusatory' के बदले 'accusatory' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'acusatory' शब्द की स्पेलिंग गलत है। सही स्पेलिंग 'accusatory' (अर्थात 'आरोपात्मक') है; जैसे— The media portrayed the incident in an accusatory tone. 'accusatory' will be used instead of 'acusatory' because the spelling of 'acusatory' is incorrect. The correct spelling is 'accusatory' (meaning 'blaming or accusing'); Like— The media portrayed the incident in an accusatory tone.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Spill the beans
options: To literally drop beans on the floor.
To reveal a secret or information that was supposed to be kept confidential.
To cook and serve beans for a meal.
To express one's love for someone.
questions.explanation: B) Spill the beans(phrase) – To reveal a secret or information that was supposed to be kept confidential भेद खोलना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the highlighted idiom Senior bankers are fish out of water when it comes to international lending
options: A person out of accustomed environment
questions.explanation: A) A fish out of water(phrase) – A person out of accustomed environmentअसहज और बेचैन स्थिति

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. At least four people died in (A) / bathing accidents in (B) / different parts in the (C) / country on Wednesday. (D)
options: At least four people died in
questions.explanation: C) 'in' के बदले 'of' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'different parts of the country' सही expression है; जैसे— People from different parts of the world attended the conference. 'of' will be used instead of 'in' because 'different parts of the country' is the correct expression; Like— People from different parts of the world attended the conference.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The biggest platforms for budding (A) / singers in today’s age (B) / are reality shows but they’re (C) / a dime in dozen. (D)
questions.explanation: D) 'a dime in dozen' के बदले'a dime a dozen'का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'a dime a dozen' एक idiom (मुहावरा) है जिसका अर्थ है 'सामान्य या आसानी से उपलब्ध'। 'a dime a dozen'will be used instead of 'a dime in dozen' because 'a dime a dozen' is an idiom which means 'common or easily available'.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. The smell would soon be followed by a whirring sound of the beans getting ground. My mother would rotate the handle of a mechanical grinder fixed on a kitchen shelf. Q. After a while, the comforting aroma of freshly brewed filter kaapi would waft in the air. R. ‘Coffee is just a drink, but kaapi is an emotion’, proclaimed an advertisement. S. Having switched to mostly masala chai and occasionally instant coffee, I remembered the post siesta afternoons in my home town and hallucinated on the aroma of roasted coffee beans.
questions.explanation: A) RSPQR starts the paragraph by introducing a quote from an advertisement: "‘Coffee is just a drink, but kaapi is an emotion’, proclaimed an advertisement." This grabs the reader’s attention and sets the tone for a nostalgic reflection on coffee. S follows R, providing a personal recollection related to the quote. The narrator remembers the aroma of roasted coffee beans and the post-siesta afternoons, setting a vivid scene that connects to the emotional impact of "kaapi" mentioned earlier. P comes next, as it gives more details about the memory. It describes the smell and sound of coffee beans being ground by the narrator's mother, further painting a sensory picture of the experience. Q concludes the paragraph by bringing the memory to life: "After a while, the comforting aroma of freshly brewed filter kaapi would waft in the air." This sentence ties together the sensory details and evokes the feeling of nostalgia described throughout the paragraph.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The Enlightenment, sometimes known as the ‘Age of Reason’, is described as the period of (1)________ scientific, political, and philosophical debate that characterised European culture (2) ________ the ‘long’ 18th century: from the late 17th century until the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815. This was an era of an enormous (3) ________ in thinking and reason, which historian Roy Porter describes as ‘decisive in the formation of modernity’. Centuries of custom and tradition were (4) ________ in favour of exploration, individuality, tolerance, and scientific (5) ________, all of which contributed to the formation of the ‘modern world’. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1..
questions.explanation: C)'Rigorous'का use होगा क्योंकि "rigorous" का अर्थ है गहन, विस्तृत और सटीक। वाक्य में उल्लेख किया गया है कि यह युग गहन वैज्ञानिक, राजनीतिक, और दार्शनिक बहसों का था, जो यूरोपीय संस्कृति की विशेषता थी। इसलिए 'rigorous' यहाँ सही है। वहीं 'Malicious' का अर्थ है द्वेषपूर्ण, जो इस संदर्भ में गलत है। 'Cautions' का अर्थ है सतर्कता या सावधानी, जो इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'Auspicious' का अर्थ है शुभ, लेकिन यह शब्द गहन और सटीक चर्चा के लिए सही नहीं बैठता। 'Rigorous'will be used because it means thorough, exhaustive, and accurate. The sentence mentions that this era was characterized by intense scientific, political, and philosophical debates, making 'rigorous' the correct choice here. Whereas, 'Malicious' means harmful or intending to do harm, which is not relevant in this context. 'Cautions' means careful or wary, which does not fit here. 'Auspicious' means favorable or conducive to success, which does not describe the intense and detailed debates during the Enlightenment.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The Enlightenment, sometimes known as the ‘Age of Reason’, is described as the period of (1)________ scientific, political, and philosophical debate that characterised European culture (2) ________ the ‘long’ 18th century: from the late 17th century until the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815. This was an era of an enormous (3) ________ in thinking and reason, which historian Roy Porter describes as ‘decisive in the formation of modernity’. Centuries of custom and tradition were (4) ________ in favour of exploration, individuality, tolerance, and scientific (5) ________, all of which contributed to the formation of the ‘modern world’. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2..
questions.explanation: C)'Throughout'का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है "पूरे समय या सीमा में।" वाक्य में यह बताया गया है कि 'Enlightenment' किस अवधि में हुआ, और 'long 18th century' के दौरान इसे संदर्भित किया गया है, इसलिए 'throughout' यहाँ सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है।'Above' का अर्थ है "ऊपर," जो समय अवधि या सांस्कृतिक विवरण से संबंधित नहीं है, इसलिए यह गलत है।'Over with' का मतलब है "किसी चीज को समाप्त करना," जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठता।'Along with' का अर्थ है "के साथ-साथ," जो समय अवधि के संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'Throughout'will be used because it means "during the entire time or extent of something." The sentence is describing the time span of the Enlightenment as occurring during the ‘long’ 18th century, so 'throughout' is the most suitable choice.'Above' means "at a higher place," which is unrelated to a time span or cultural description, and thus incorrect here.'Over with' implies "completed or finished with something," which does not align with the sentence's meaning. 'Along with' suggests "in addition to," which does not fit the context of describing a time period.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The Enlightenment, sometimes known as the ‘Age of Reason’, is described as the period of (1)________ scientific, political, and philosophical debate that characterised European culture (2) ________ the ‘long’ 18th century: from the late 17th century until the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815. This was an era of an enormous (3) ________ in thinking and reason, which historian Roy Porter describes as ‘decisive in the formation of modernity’. Centuries of custom and tradition were (4) ________ in favour of exploration, individuality, tolerance, and scientific (5) ________, all of which contributed to the formation of the ‘modern world’. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3..
questions.explanation: C)Shift'का use होगा क्योंकि "shift" का अर्थ होता है बदलाव या परिवर्तन, और passage में यह बताया गया है कि उस युग में सोच और तर्क में एक बहुत बड़ा बदलाव या परिवर्तन हुआ। 'Secure' का अर्थ है सुरक्षित करना, जो यहाँ contextually उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'Embed' का अर्थ है जड़ जमाना या स्थापित करना, जो यहाँ फिट नहीं होता। 'Shield' का अर्थ है रक्षा करना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। इसलिए 'Shift' सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है। 'Shift'will be used because it means a change or transformation, and the passage refers to a significant change in thinking and reasoning during that era. 'Secure' means to make safe, which is not contextually appropriate here. 'Embed' means to fix or establish firmly, which doesn't fit. 'Shield' implies protection, which is not relevant in this context. Thus, 'Shift' is the most suitable answer.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The Enlightenment, sometimes known as the ‘Age of Reason’, is described as the period of (1)________ scientific, political, and philosophical debate that characterised European culture (2) ________ the ‘long’ 18th century: from the late 17th century until the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815. This was an era of an enormous (3) ________ in thinking and reason, which historian Roy Porter describes as ‘decisive in the formation of modernity’. Centuries of custom and tradition were (4) ________ in favour of exploration, individuality, tolerance, and scientific (5) ________, all of which contributed to the formation of the ‘modern world’. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: C)'Discarded'का use होगा क्योंकि 'discarded' का अर्थ होता है किसी वस्तु, विचार, या परंपरा को त्याग देना। यह वाक्य यह व्यक्त कर रहा है कि सदियों पुरानी परंपराएं और रीति-रिवाजों को त्याग कर नए विचारों, अन्वेषण, और विज्ञान को अपनाया गया। 'Defrosted' का अर्थ होता है जमे हुए को पिघलाना, जो इस संदर्भ में अप्रासंगिक है। 'Dejected' का अर्थ है निराश होना, जो वाक्य के भाव से मेल नहीं खाता। 'Demean' का अर्थ है अपमानित करना, जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं होता। 'Discarded'will be used because it means to give up or abandon something, especially ideas, traditions, or practices. The sentence implies that centuries of customs and traditions were abandoned to make way for exploration, individuality, tolerance, and scientific advancement, making 'discarded' the most appropriate choice here. 'Defrosted' means thawed or unfrozen, which is irrelevant in this context. 'Dejected' means feeling sad or disheartened, which doesn't fit the sentence. 'Demean' means to insult or degrade, which is also not contextually appropriate.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The Enlightenment, sometimes known as the ‘Age of Reason’, is described as the period of (1)________ scientific, political, and philosophical debate that characterised European culture (2) ________ the ‘long’ 18th century: from the late 17th century until the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815. This was an era of an enormous (3) ________ in thinking and reason, which historian Roy Porter describes as ‘decisive in the formation of modernity’. Centuries of custom and tradition were (4) ________ in favour of exploration, individuality, tolerance, and scientific (5) ________, all of which contributed to the formation of the ‘modern world’. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5..
questions.explanation: C)'Endeavour'का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ होता है "कोशिश" या "प्रयास"। passage में यह उल्लेख किया गया है कि यह युग व्यक्तिगतता, सहिष्णुता और वैज्ञानिक प्रयासों के पक्ष में था, जिसने आधुनिक दुनिया के निर्माण में योगदान दिया। इसलिए 'endeavour' यहाँ सही विकल्प है। 'Shirk' का अर्थ है "टालना" या "दायित्वों से बचना", जो यहाँ फिट नहीं होता। 'Doodle' का अर्थ है "अनावश्यक रूप से लिखना या रेखाचित्र बनाना", जो इस संदर्भ में अनुपयुक्त है। 'Slacken' का अर्थ है "धीमा करना" या "कमज़ोर करना", जो passage की भावना से मेल नहीं खाता। Endeavour'will be used because it means "an attempt or effort." The passage highlights that this era was inclined towards individuality, tolerance, and scientific endeavours, contributing to the formation of the modern world, making 'endeavour' the most suitable choice. 'Shirk' means "to evade or avoid duties," which does not fit here. 'Doodle' means "to scribble aimlessly," which is irrelevant in this context. 'Slacken' means "to slow down or weaken," which is contrary to the spirit of the passage.
